result.summary: Analyzed sub_layer_0_05_rules: 104+ rule files across organized directory structure with comprehensive governance

0_every_api_request.description: Rules that apply to EVERY API request universally
rules: AI_CONTEXT_MODIFICATION_PROTOCOL.md - Show diagram, wait for approval before modifying AI context
AI_CONTEXT_COMMIT_PUSH_RULE.md - Git add/commit/push after approved AI context changes
CONTEXT_TRAVERSAL_RULE.md - Read CLAUDE.md files and gather context before starting work

1_scenario_based.description: Rules that apply only in specific scenarios
rules: safety_governance.md - Security decisions, permission escalations, sensitive operations
LAYER_CONTEXT_HEADER_PROTOCOL.md - Creating new files that need layer/stage headers

0_instruction_docs.description: Additional instruction documents covering terminal execution, agents, testing, and integrations

coreSecurityPrinciples: Least Privilege - Agents operate with minimum permissions needed
Defense in Depth - Multiple layers of protection (filesystem, network, commands)
Audit Everything - All actions logged for review
Human Oversight - Critical decisions require approval
Fail Secure - When in doubt, deny and escalate

permissionLevels.Layer0_SystemManager: Full filesystem within workspace, all commands with approval gates
permissionLevels.Layer1_ProjectManager: Project files, dependencies with approval, feature branch git ops
permissionLevels.Layer2_StandardAgent: Feature files, safe commands, limited API calls
permissionLevels.Layer3_SandboxedWrite: Component directories only, whitelisted commands only

governanceSummary.emergencyProcedures: Emergency stop protocol with severity levels P0-P3

directoryStructure.0_every_api_request: Rules for EVERY interaction (include in prompts)
directoryStructure.1_scenario_based: Rules for specific scenarios (reference, don't include)
